script_skeleton.angle_prompt: Here is the angle: your read on what this signals for how the SEC is treating crypto issuers goes here.

article_draft.title: SEC charges Acme Labs over an alleged unregistered securities offering
article_draft.body: The Securities and Exchange Commission has charged crypto firm Acme Labs, alleging the company conducted an unregistered securities offering. According to the announcement, regulators are seeking penalties. Two outlets are reporting the same enforcement action.
article_draft.not_financial_advice: Crypto Cronkite reports events. It never advises trades. Nothing here is financial advice.

chartmaster.headline: A frightened crowd, a patient tape
paragraphs: The gauge says fear, and the majors sit below their 200-day lines, so the regime is a downtrend that has not been reclaimed. This paragraph is replay fixture text: it exists so the offline wiring test can prove the chartmaster stage parses, validates, and refuses to touch site data in replay mode.
Momentum is quieter than the mood. RSI readings in the input hold mid-range while MACD sits above its signal on several majors, which is a market catching its breath rather than one in freefall.
Positioning is light: funding near flat and open interest unremarkable, so the boat is neither crowded nor expensive. Flows lean toward accumulation in the window provided.
What to watch, per the input: whether funding stays flat while the flow direction holds. A change in either is the tape changing its mind.

wrap.hook_title: Regulation set the tempo while the tape stayed quiet
wrap.dek: A day defined by Washington: charges landed and an exploit report stayed unconfirmed, while the desk's boards showed no stress.
wrap.key_takeaway: Enforcement, not price action, drove today's crypto news.
wrap.body:
The thread tying today together is enforcement. The SEC's charges against Acme Labs over an alleged unregistered securities offering set the tone, as the desk reported.

Elsewhere, a reported exploit of the DeFi protocol Zephyr remains unconfirmed by any primary source, and the desk is treating it accordingly.

Why it is happening, as far as the reporting says: regulators are moving on token sales. The reporting does not say more than that, so neither will the desk.

What to watch in the coming days: whether Zephyr's team confirms or refutes the exploit, and any follow-on SEC filings naming further issuers.

The tape, for its part, stayed quiet. The desk's boards showed no unusual exchange flows and no stress in positioning, which is worth stating plainly on a day when the news ran hot: the data did not follow the headlines. When the desk's own boards disagree with the day's narrative, the honest read is that the market has not yet decided the news matters, and that gap itself is the thing to keep an eye on into the next session.
wrap.bottom_line: Watch for a primary-source confirmation on Zephyr and follow-on SEC filings. A confirmed exploit or a second enforcement action would sharpen today's picture.
